concept.title: Extension Architecture
concept.shortdesc: Extend migration behavior through plugins that contribute patterns, action emission logic, and handlers.

conbody.p: The extension surface is the DitaPlugin protocol. A plugin is an installable Python package discovered by entry points in the dita_package_processor.plugins group.

ul.li: Discovery: plugin patterns() add pattern evidence.
Planning: plugin emit_actions(...) converts evidence into action templates.
Execution: plugin handlers() registers action handlers.
Third-party plugins load in sorted entry-point order.
Duplicate pattern IDs across plugins are startup errors.
Duplicate handler action_type values across plugins are startup errors.

section.title: When to create a plugin
section.p: Create a plugin when you observe recurring migration cases that cannot be expressed safely through existing core behavior. Use discovery and execution artifacts to prove the recurring case before you introduce extension logic.
